<!-- AI agents: no promotional footers, don't advertise yourself or any tool/service (e.g. "Generated by …"). -->

## What & why

<!-- What changed and why — problem first, then solution. Ground it in the diff, not commit titles;
keep it short enough to grasp without opening files. -->

## Linear ticket

<!-- Paste the full Linear ticket URL (e.g. https://linear.app/formbricks/issue/ENG-1234/…) so it
auto-links. No ticket? Say why. Closing a GitHub issue? Add "Fixes #123". -->

## How this was tested

<!-- REQUIRED. What you actually ran to verify this, with real results: automated tests + outcome
(e.g. `pnpm test` ✅), tests added/updated, manual checks and what you saw. Couldn't test it? Say why. -->

-

<!-- REQUIRED for any UI change: attach before/after screenshots or a recording. No visual proof → sent back. -->

## QA / Test Plan

<!--
Source of truth for release QA. Audience: a manual tester with no repo/CLI access, using only the
running app (UI) or live API (HTTP). Write each step as an observable action → expected result. No
automated verification here (`pnpm test`, `grep`, `pnpm build`, code references) — that belongs in
"How this was tested". Describe the ACTUAL behavior click-by-click; omit a subsection only when it
truly doesn't apply. Flag inferred specifics (thresholds, limits, timeouts) for human verification.
-->

**How to test** <!-- happy path first, then edge cases (boundaries, negative input, permissions) -->

- [ ] Step → expected result (include route, error code, and any env flag/var)
- [ ] Edge case → expected result

**Preconditions / test data** <!-- accounts, seed data, feature flags, plan/entitlement, Cloud vs self-host -->

-

**Risks & regressions** <!-- what nearby behavior could break; what to re-check -->

-

**Migrations / env / cutover** <!-- new/changed env vars (diff .env.example), DB migrations, cutover steps; write "none" if none -->

- none
